name: PR-CV 202: San Cristóbal Trail
name_original: Ruta de San Cristóbal (PR-CV 202) — Circular al Santuario de La Mare de Deu dels Angels
description: Hike the moderate 9.5-mile Route of San Cristóbal (PR-CV 202) to the historic Sanctuary of La Mare de Déu dels Àngels for panoramic views.
region_intro: Hiking around Sant Mateu offers diverse landscapes characterized by Mediterranean flora and ancient agricultural practices. The region features extensive millenary olive groves and traditional dry stone constructions, reflecting historical land management. Trails often lead through tranquil natural areas like Paraje Natural Municipal Racó del Frare, providing a blend of natural beauty and historical context. The area's varied terrain includes gentle paths and moderate climbs, suitable for exploring its natural and cultural heritage. Best hiking trails around Sant Mateu The most popular hiking route is PR-CV 202: San Cristóbal Trail, a 9.5 miles (15.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 19 minutes to complete. This moderate route leads to the historic Sanctuary of La Mare de Déu dels Àngels, offering panoramic views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Racó del Frare loop from Paratge Natural Municipal Racó del Frare, a moderate 5.5 miles (8.8 km) path. This trail explores the natural beauty of the Racó del Frare municipal park. Local hikers also love the Hiking loop from Sant Mateu, a 4.5 miles (7.3 km) trail leading through the local landscape, often completed in about 2 hours 7 minutes. Hiking around Sant Mateu is defined by Mediterranean flora, millenary olive groves, and historical dry stone constructions. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging moderate hikes. The routes in Sant Mateu are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 150 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Sant Mateu's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Sant Mateu?
A: Sant Mateu offers a diverse network of over 20 hiking trails, catering to various fitness levels. You'll find a mix of easy walks and more challenging moderate hikes, allowing you to explore the region's natural beauty and historical heritage.

Q: What kind of landscapes and natural features can I expect to see while hiking in Sant Mateu?
A: The hiking trails in Sant Mateu showcase a rich Mediterranean landscape. You'll encounter extensive &lt;strong&gt;millenary olive groves&lt;/strong&gt;, some thousands of years old, and traditional &lt;strong&gt;dry stone constructions&lt;/strong&gt;. Many routes pass through tranquil natural areas like the Paraje Natural Municipal Racó del Frare, offering vibrant flora and opportunities to observe local wildlife.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking routes in Sant Mateu?
A: Yes, Sant Mateu has 8 easy trails perfect for families or those seeking a relaxed walk. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17509855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Millennial Olive Grove loop from Sant Mateu&lt;/a&gt;, which is 5.2 miles (8.3 km) long and takes you through ancient olive groves. Another easy choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38238228" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corral de Parra loop from Sant Mateu&lt;/a&gt;, a 3.3-mile (5.3 km) route.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I discover along the hiking trails?
A: Many trails in Sant Mateu lead to significant historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e878908750/pr-cv-202-san-cristobal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;PR-CV 202: San Cristóbal Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, takes you to the historic Sanctuary of La Mare de Déu dels Àngels, an architectural marvel with panoramic views. You can also find sections of the ancient Roman road, Via Augusta, and traditional 'assegadors' along various routes. Other notable points of interest include St. Christopher's Hermitage and The Old Mill, dating back to the 15th century.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Sant Mateu?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Sant Mateu are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38237900" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Racó del Frare loop from Paratge Natural Municipal Racó del Frare&lt;/a&gt;, which explores a beautiful municipal park, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19814703" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Sant Mateu&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a comprehensive tour of the local landscape.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Sant Mateu?
A: The trails in Sant Mateu are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 150 reviews. Hikers often praise the well-maintained paths, the unique blend of natural beauty—especially the ancient olive groves—and the rich historical context found along the routes. Many appreciate the diverse terrain suitable for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots accessible via hiking?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e878908750/pr-cv-202-san-cristobal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;PR-CV 202: San Cristóbal Trail&lt;/a&gt; leads to the Sanctuary of La Mare de Déu dels Àngels, which offers breathtaking panoramic views of the surrounding landscape. Additionally, the region's varied terrain often provides elevated spots with scenic vistas of the Mediterranean flora and historical agricultural areas.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Sant Mateu?
A: Sant Mateu is ideal for hiking during the spring and autumn months when the weather is mild and comfortable. These seasons offer pleasant temperatures for exploring the trails and enjoying the vibrant Mediterranean flora. While summer can be warm, early mornings or late afternoons are still suitable for shorter, easier walks.

Q: Are there any natural monuments or unique geological features to see?
A: Yes, the area around Sant Mateu features several natural monuments. You can visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3951882" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Millennial Olive Tree of the Four Legs (Canet lo Roig)&lt;/a&gt;, a truly ancient natural wonder. Other notable features include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/329152" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mola de Xert&lt;/a&gt; and the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6337909" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font de l'Aubelló&lt;/a&gt;, offering unique natural beauty.

Q: Is parking available near the trailheads in Sant Mateu?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in and around Sant Mateu, particularly near popular trailheads and the town center. Many routes, especially those starting directly from Sant Mateu, offer convenient access to parking facilities, making it easy to begin your hike.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Sant Mateu?
A: Many trails in Sant Mateu are dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the outdoors with your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in natural areas, to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Please also be mindful of local regulations and clean up after your pet.

Q: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the hiking routes for a post-hike meal?
A: Sant Mateu, as a historic town, offers several cafes and restaurants where you can enjoy a meal or refreshment after your hike. The town center is easily accessible from many of the local trails, providing a convenient spot to relax and refuel with traditional Valencian cuisine.
tips:
Its construction dates from the 16th to the 18th centuries and the complex is made up of the church, the inn, the viewpoint, the well and other adjoining buildings.
It has a very particular architectural style, a Renaissance combination with baroque decoration from the 17th century.
A very beautiful and picturesque place to visit.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike the Route of San Cristóbal (PR-CV 202)?
faqs.answer: This moderate circular hike usually takes around 4 hours and 19 minutes to complete. This duration is for the full 15.3-kilometer route.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The Route of San Cristóbal (PR-CV 202) is rated as a moderate hike. While not overly strenuous, it covers approximately 15.3 kilometers with an elevation gain of about 338 meters. Hikers should possess a good physical condition to comfortably enjoy the trail.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The trail traverses diverse landscapes, including Mediterranean scrubland and pine forests. The path is generally accessible, though some sections may require firm footing. As you ascend, you'll find breathtaking panoramic viewpoints.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Route of San Cristóbal?
faqs.answer: The primary destination is the historic Hermitage of Our Lady of the Angels, also known as the Sanctuary of La Mare de Déu dels Àngels. This sanctuary offers exceptional 360-degree views. The route also provides panoramic vistas over Girona, the distant Pyrenees, and the Pla de Girona.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Spring is an excellent time to hike this route, as it brings vibrant wildflowers to the diverse plant species found along the trail. The weather is generally pleasant for hiking during this season.

faqs.question: Is the Route of San Cristóbal (PR-CV 202) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information regarding specific dog restrictions for this trail is not provided. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or other hikers, and to carry waste bags.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: There is no mention of permits or entrance fees required to hike the Route of San Cristóbal (PR-CV 202) or to access the Sanctuary of La Mare de Déu dels Àngels.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the Sanctuary of La Mare de Déu dels Àngels?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Sanctuary of La Mare de Déu dels Àngels, which is the trail's destination, includes a guesthouse and a restaurant, offering options for refreshments or an overnight stay.

faqs.question: What makes the Sanctuary of La Mare de Déu dels Àngels particularly unique?
faqs.answer: Beyond its historical significance dating back to 1409 and its stunning views, the sanctuary holds a unique cultural anecdote: it was the secret wedding location for the renowned surrealist artist Salvador Dalí and Gala in 1958.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The Route of San Cristóbal (PR-CV 202) is a circular trail, typically starting and ending in the same location. While specific parking details aren't provided, trailheads for circular routes often have designated parking areas nearby. It's recommended to check local maps or guides for the most convenient starting point and parking options.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect from the Sanctuary of La Mare de Déu dels Àngels?
faqs.answer: From the Sanctuary, situated on Puig Alt (483 meters), you can expect exceptional 360-degree panoramic views. These vistas include the Eastern Pyrenees, the sea, Montseny, and the regions of Gironès and La Selva.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of Our Lady of the Angels
